source_label: Wiktionary — chute: US /ʃut/, from French chute (Old French cheüe, Vulgar Latin cadūta)
notes: Chutes is Rayon Labs' serverless AI inference platform on Bittensor Subnet 64, and one of the highest-throughput providers routable from OpenRouter. The name is the ordinary English word chute (as in parachute or a garbage chute), borrowed from French, so the ch is /ʃ/ and it sounds exactly like shoots. Non-native developers reading it cold in a provider list routinely say chootz with an English ch, which is wrong.
